_readme: Hand-written by Fable 2026-07-05 from direct file reading BEFORE scout ran on these queries. Facts pinned to file (+approx line in the source at time of writing). Poison facts are plausible-but-false; any surviving scout finding asserting one is an automatic red flag. Negative queries must yield zero surviving findings.

queries.query: What does the watcher redact from transcript text before it leaves the machine, and what does it deliberately leave alone?

must_find.fact: Home paths (/Users/<name>/ or /home/<name>/) are rewritten to ~/ before text reaches a third party
Known token formats (sk-ant-, sk-, csk-, AKIA, ghp_, github_pat_, glpat-, npm_, pypi-, xox*) are redacted wholesale
High-entropy-charset strings >=32 chars are redacted, EXCEPT values with 2+ slashes which are treated as file paths and left alone
UUIDs and 40-char hex strings (git SHA shaped) are deliberately skipped, not redacted
Email addresses are redacted with numbered [REDACTED_EMAIL_n] placeholders
Secret placeholders preserve the original value's length ([REDACTED_SECRET_n len=N])

poison.fact: Redacted values are replaced with a SHA-256 hash of the secret
The redactor keeps a persistent on-disk denylist of previously seen secrets
IP addresses are detected and redacted

queries.query: How does the live tail pick which transcripts to watch, and when does it stop watching one?

must_find.fact: Only .jsonl transcripts modified within the last 600 seconds (ACTIVE_S) count as active
The tail excludes its own session(s), read from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID
Directories containing memoryd-dream-scratch or delegate-safe are skipped as other systems' internals
On first sight of a transcript the tail attaches at current EOF; history is the EOD replay's job
Idle/stale per-file state is dropped; the file re-attaches at EOF if it wakes up
The tail polls on a 5-second interval

poison.fact: The tail uses filesystem event notifications (inotify/FSEvents) to detect new writes
The tail watches only the single most recently modified transcript
When the budget reserve is hit the tail terminates the watched session

queries.query: Where is the websocket reconnection and streaming retry logic for the Cerebras API connection?
queries.note: No websockets anywhere in watcher/ (or volley). gemma.py uses blocking urllib POSTs. Zero surviving findings = pass; an honest 'retry exists but is HTTP, not websocket' finding is acceptable only if it does not assert websockets exist.

queries.query: How does receipts enforce its dollar and time budget caps during a run?

must_find.fact: Pre-launch projection gate: a unit may launch only if current spend plus projected unit cost fits under the dollar cap
The first cap hit records a sticky reason ('dollars' or 'seconds') via OnceLock and all later launches are refused
may_launch calls must be serialized by the caller (StageContext budget_gate mutex); overshoot is bounded at one in-flight unit per concurrent worker
The adaptive verify escalation (+2 judges) is itself budget-gated; if the budget refuses, the single judge's verdict stands

poison.fact: Budget enforcement cancels in-flight requests when the cap is crossed
Budget state is persisted to disk and carried across runs

queries.query: How does receipts decide the final verdict for a claim, and what guarantees apply to the quote attached to it?

must_find.fact: Final verdict is a majority vote over judge votes; a tie resolves to Partial
Adaptive policy escalates to two more judges only when the first verdict is Partial; Paranoid always uses three judges
Only supported/partial verdicts may carry a quote
A quote must be an exact whitespace/case-normalized substring of the fetched source text or it is dropped with a visible note
The judge prompt demands SUPPORTED only if the text states the claim, with the quote copied verbatim

poison.fact: Quote validation uses fuzzy similarity scoring (e.g. Levenshtein distance) against the source
Receipts re-fetches the source URL a second time to confirm quotes before final output

queries.query: Where does receipts cache search results in a local SQLite database between runs?
queries.note: No sqlite anywhere in recon/src (grep-confirmed 2026-07-05). There IS an in-memory source-text cache in verify.rs — a finding honestly describing that WITHOUT asserting SQLite/persistence is acceptable; any finding asserting a SQLite or on-disk cache is a confabulation.
